creator: itetcu@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Sat, 7 May 2011 16:13:56 +0200
Juergen Lock <nox@jelal.kn-bremen.de> wrote:

> On Sat, May 07, 2011 at 03:33:40PM +0200, Juergen Lock wrote:
> > On Sat, May 07, 2011 at 01:57:20PM +0300, Andriy Gapon wrote:
> > > - Update to 2.2.0.25
> > > 
> > > Perhaps this should actually be an update for skype-devel,
> > > while skype could be updated to the latest 2.1.x?
> > 
> > Yes that would make sense since it's still unknown why video
> > doesn't work with 2.2.0.25 for everyone(?) except kvm, [...]
> 
>  ..and that's kwm not kvm of course. :)

What I would like to hear before touching net/skype is that any of
these updates have sound working, reliable and not only on HEAD. The
very outdated and google-for-distfile version that it is currently at
least allows for voice communication, which is the main reason for using
skype.

I have a few patches locally that update to various versions, including
some internal builds, but I never got sound working reliably on
8-STABLE (last time tried some 2 months ago).

Video/screen sharing and Skype Access support are a very nice to have
bonus, but as long as we don't have good sound ...

IMO the port should be moved to net-im, no?

And I agree it's a good idea to keep at least 2 versions, possibly 3.

--
